name: leash-apply

containerEnv.//proxy: Default egress route: the squid hostname-allowlist proxy started by init-firewall.sh. CLIs (gh, git, curl) honor these and so reach GitHub regardless of its CDN IP rotation. Set in both cases so a tool that reads only one form still routes correctly.

//postStart: Re-arm the egress firewall on every container START (not just create). postCreateCommand runs once at creation; a plain restart (Docker/daemon/machine reboot) skips it, leaving squid dead and iptables wiped while HTTPS_PROXY still points at :3128 — proxied clients then fail with ECONNREFUSED. init-firewall.sh is idempotent, so re-running it here is safe. Critically for apply: a wiped iptables also means egress is briefly UNFILTERED until this re-arms it.
